records.ety: [L. relegatus, p. p. of relegare; pref. re- re- + legare to send with a commission or charge. See Legate.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Relegated (-g?`t?d); p. pr. & vb. n. Relegating.]

defs: To remove, usually to an inferior position; to consign; to transfer; specifically, to send into exile; to banish.
quotes: It [the Latin language] was relegated into the study of the scholar.
